aadhaar.overview: You can update your Aadhaar address online using the UIDAI Self Service Update Portal if your mobile number is linked with Aadhaar.
steps: Visit the UIDAI Self Service Update Portal.
Click on 'Update Address in Aadhaar'.
Log in using your Aadhaar number and OTP sent to your registered mobile number.
Enter your new address details.
Upload a valid Proof of Address document.
Submit the request and note the Update Request Number (URN) to track status.
documents: Valid Proof of Address (POA)
Examples: Passport, Driving License, Bank Statement, Utility Bill

passport.overview: Indian passports are issued by the Ministry of External Affairs through Passport Seva.
steps: Visit the Passport Seva portal.
Register for a new account.
Log in and select 'Apply for Fresh Passport'.
Fill the application form online.
Pay the passport application fee.
Schedule an appointment at a Passport Seva Kendra (PSK).
Visit the PSK with required documents.
Police verification may follow before passport issuance.
documents: Proof of identity
Proof of address
Birth certificate or date of birth proof

pan.overview: A PAN (Permanent Account Number) card is issued by the Income Tax Department. You can apply online through the NSDL (Protean) or UTIITSL portals. Indian citizens use Form 49A. The fee is approximately Rs. 107 for Indian address dispatch.
steps: Visit the NSDL (Protean) portal or UTIITSL portal.
Select 'Apply for New PAN Card (Form 49A)' for Indian citizens.
Choose your category (Individual, HUF, Firm, etc.).
Select document submission mode: Digital (paperless) or Physical.
Fill in personal details, contact details, and AO code.
Upload required documents (identity proof, address proof, date of birth proof).
Pay the application fee online (debit/credit card, net banking, or UPI).
Note the acknowledgement number to track your application status.
PAN card will be dispatched to your address within 15-20 working days.
documents: Proof of Identity: Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Passport, or Driving License
Proof of Address: Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Utility Bill, or Bank Statement
Proof of Date of Birth: Birth certificate, SSLC mark sheet, or Passport
Passport-size photograph

driving_license.overview: Driving licenses in India are issued by the Regional Transport Office (RTO) through the Parivahan Sarathi portal. You must first obtain a Learner's License, wait at least 30 days, then apply for a permanent Driving License after passing a driving test.
steps: Visit the Sarathi Parivahan portal at https://sarathi.parivahan.gov.in.
Select your state.
Click 'Apply for Learner License' and read the instructions.
Fill in personal details and verify using Aadhaar OTP.
Upload required documents and pay the fee (Rs. 150-200).
Take the online Learner's License test on traffic rules and road signs.
If you pass, download your Learner's License (valid for 6 months).
After 30 days, apply for permanent Driving License on the same portal.
Book a driving test slot at your nearest RTO.
Visit the RTO with original documents and pass the driving test.
Permanent Driving License will be dispatched to your address within 7-21 days.
documents: Proof of Age: Birth certificate, SSLC mark sheet, or Passport
Proof of Address: Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Utility Bill, or Passport
Passport-size photographs
Medical Certificate: Form 1A (for age 40 and above) or Form 1 (below 40)
Learner's License (for permanent DL application)

rti.overview: The Right to Information (RTI) Act 2005 empowers any Indian citizen to request information from Central and State government departments. For Central Government ministries, you can file RTI applications online through the official RTI portal. The application fee is Rs. 10. Public authorities must respond within 30 days.
steps: Visit the RTI Online portal at https://rtionline.gov.in.
Click on 'Submit Request'.
Fill in your personal details: name, address, email, and phone number.
Select the Ministry or Department you want information from.
Write your RTI request clearly, specifying exactly what information you need.
Upload any supporting documents if needed (optional).
Pay the Rs. 10 application fee via internet banking, debit/credit card, UPI, or RuPay.
Submit the application and note your registration number.
Track your application status using the registration number on the same portal.
The concerned Public Information Officer (PIO) must respond within 30 days.
documents: No specific documents required for filing
Identity proof like Aadhaar or Voter ID (for portal registration)
BPL certificate (if applicable, for fee exemption)
Valid email ID and mobile number

ration_card.overview: A Ration Card is issued by State Governments through the Department of Food, Civil Supplies and Consumer Affairs. It enables eligible households to purchase subsidized food grains under the Public Distribution System (PDS). It also serves as valid identity and address proof. The application process varies by state but can generally be done online through your state's food supply portal.
steps: Visit your state's official Department of Food, Supplies and Consumer Affairs website.
You can find state-wise links at https://nfsa.gov.in.
Register on the portal using your mobile number and create a login ID.
Select 'Apply for New Ration Card' or 'Request New Ration Card'.
Fill in family details including head of family, all family members, income, and address.
Upload scanned copies of required documents (Aadhaar, address proof, income proof).
Submit the application and download the acknowledgement receipt.
Track status online using your application reference number.
A field verification may be conducted by a government officer at your residence.
Once verified, the ration card will be issued and sinked to your nearest Fair Price Shop.
documents: Aadhaar card of head of family and all family members
Proof of Address: Electricity bill, water bill, gas connection receipt, or rent agreement
Income certificate or self-declaration of annual income
Passport-size photographs of the head of family
Bank account details
